description: Sarah's 4-qubit entanglement circuit reimplementation + first-row snake displacement bug fix

tech_stack.frontend: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind 4 + ahooks + react-query + Vitest
tech_stack.backend: n/a (client-side game; calls Quokka quantum simulator HTTP API)
tech_stack.infrastructure: GitHub Pages (auto-deploy via .github/workflows/deploy.yml on push to main)

architecture_decisions.decision: Expand biased Bell strategy to 4 qubits (q0/q1 biased pair for visibility, q2/q3 pure Bell pair for type)
architecture_decisions.rationale: Sarah's full design separates two concerns: q0/q1 decide whether each tile of an entangled pair appears (independent flags with biased marginals), q2/q3 in a |Φ+⟩ Bell state decide snake/ladder type with perfect correlation so both tiles are always the same type
alternatives_considered: Keep 2-qubit circuit and reinterpret bits — rejected because actual cirq notebook screenshot shows 4 qubits
Run separate 2-qubit measurements per tile — rejected because partner type must be Bell-correlated with player tile

architecture_decisions.decision: Partner tile is preset to collapsed state ('snake'/'ladder'/'interference') at first measurement instead of staying uncollapsed
architecture_decisions.rationale: With 4-qubit circuit, m2 already determines both tiles' type at first measurement (Bell correlation). Re-running the 4-qubit circuit when partner is later visited would discard that information. Presetting partner to its determined type lets use-play.ts's existing 'collapsed qubit landed-on' branch fire correctly.
alternatives_considered: Leave partner uncollapsed and re-measure on visit — rejected: breaks Bell correlation

architecture_decisions.decision: Snake outcome must guarantee newCell < targetCell (and ladder must guarantee newCell > targetCell)
architecture_decisions.rationale: Current computeDisplacement clamps row at edges (row 0 for snake, row 9 for ladder), so dy can become 0 and dx-only motion can move the player UP after a snake (e.g., 6 → 8). Fix: post-check and fall back to safe value.
alternatives_considered: Re-roll dice until valid — rejected: nondeterministic and complicates animations
Disallow entangled qubits in row 0 — rejected: too restrictive

tasks.title: Implement Sarah's 4-qubit biased Bell circuit + split semantics
tasks.description: Update src/lib/entanglement-strategy.ts, src/hooks/use-collapse.ts, src/types/game.ts (if needed), and src/constants/board.ts to implement Sarah's full 4-qubit circuit. q0/q1 retain the existing biased gates (Ry(1.580) q[1], Ry(0.862) q[0], Z q[1], Rz(0.720) q[0], H q[0], CNOT q[0]→q[1]) and now solely decide visibility flags per tile. q2/q3 form a fresh Bell pair (H q[2], CNOT q[2]→q[3]) deciding the shared snake/ladder type. parseResult must take 4 measurement bits and return { playerOutcome, partnerOutcome } where: m0=0 → player tile fires (type from m2), m0=1 → player tile cancelled (interference); m1=0 → partner preset to type from m2 (collapsed snake or ladder), m1=1 → partner cancelled (interference). Bit-ordering convention: result[0][i] = c[i] = q[i] measurement. Update describe()/log lines so Quokka log shows the 4-qubit circuit and explains the new mapping. Keep basicBellStrategy unchanged in semantics but its parseResult must accept the wider signature (extra bits ignored).
acceptance_criteria: Quokka receives a 4-qubit QASM with q0/q1 biased pair and q2/q3 H+CNOT pair
parseResult returns playerOutcome based on m0+m2 and partnerOutcome based on m1+m2
When m0=0 the player slides per snake/ladder (type=m2)
When m0=1 player tile is interference (no movement) but partner can still be set per m1
When m1=0 the partner qubit is immediately collapsed to 'snake' or 'ladder' (matching m2) so the next player to land triggers the existing collapsed-tile branch in use-play.ts
When m1=1 partner is collapsed to 'interference'
EntanglementStrategy.parseResult signature accepts 4 measurement bits (m2/m3 optional for basicBell)
Quokka log shows: full 4-qubit QASM, circuit diagram, mapping description (q0=player visible, q1=partner visible, q2=q3=type)
Existing use-game.test.tsx still passes (non-entangled qubit flow unaffected)

tasks.title: Fix first-row snake placing player at higher cell (and symmetric top-row ladder bug)
tasks.description: src/lib/game-helpers.ts:computeDisplacement — when targetCell is on row 0 and outcome is 'snake', dy gets clamped to 0 by clamp(coord.row + dy, 0, 9). Then a positive dx still moves the player to a higher cell number on row 0 (e.g., 6 → 8). Same symmetry bug likely exists for ladder on row 9. Fix: after computing newCell, if outcome === 'snake' && newCell >= targetCell, fall back to max(1, targetCell - 1). If outcome === 'ladder' && newCell <= targetCell, fall back to min(TOTAL_CELLS, targetCell + 1). Add log entries explaining the clamp ('Snake clamped: would have moved to N which is not lower than M'). Add unit tests covering: row-0 snake from cell 1, 5, 10; row-9 ladder from cell 91, 95, 100; mid-board cases unaffected.
acceptance_criteria: computeDisplacement returns newCell < targetCell whenever outcome === 'snake'
computeDisplacement returns newCell > targetCell whenever outcome === 'ladder' (when targetCell < TOTAL_CELLS)
Fallback clamp is logged via addLog so the Quokka log explains the adjustment
New unit tests in src/lib/__tests__/game-helpers.test.ts (or hooks/__tests__) covering row-0 snake and row-9 ladder edge cases
bun run test passes (existing + new tests)

performance: 4-qubit measurement adds 2 qubits; Quokka latency unchanged

testing_strategy.unit_tests: vitest — new tests for computeDisplacement edge cases; existing use-game.test.tsx must remain green
testing_strategy.integration_tests: manual: roll a die that lands on entangled cell, verify Quokka log shows 4-qubit circuit + correct partner preset behavior
